meaning: Alif Lam Ra (special letters whose full meaning is known to Allah)
for the ones who reject/cover up truth
and they seek it wrongfully (referring to crookedness in Allah's path)
those (referring to the disbelievers mentioned before who prefer worldly life)
a complete state of being lost (in this ayah: describing how far they have strayed)
and not/and what (connects to previous statement while introducing a negative)
Moses (the prophet sent to free the Children of Israel)
from the many different kinds of darkness
they afflict you all (referring to the Children of Israel)
your sons (being addressed to many people)
your women (referring to the women of the Israelites being addressed)
I will most certainly increase you all
Moses (the prophet who spoke these words)
we rejected/denied (what was sent to us)
from what (that you call us to)
and he postpones you all (with emphasis)
except (what comes after this word is excluded from what was mentioned before)
from what (that our forefathers used to worship)
with any clear authority/proof (that proves what we say)
upon/on (shows something is above or happening to something else)
for us/to us (referring to something being given or belonging to us)
we come/bring to you all (with emphasis)
that not (introduces what we have no reason for)
and we will most certainly be patient
so let him put complete trust in
we will most definitely expel you all
you will most definitely return (threat from the disbelievers to their messengers)
and we will most definitely make you all live somewhere
he forces himself to gulp it (referring to the punishment mentioned earlier)
their works/deeds (referring to those who disbelieved in their Lord)
he causes you all to go away
that (pointing to something far and masculine)
then he said (introducing what the weak ones said to the arrogant ones)
they acted proudly (the leaders speaking to the weak followers)
you all (masculine plural, addressing the arrogant ones)
those who free from need (with emphasis)
we would have guided you all
or (connecting two equal alternatives in a question)
any place to move away to
So I broke my promise to you all
so do not (command to stop blaming me)
you all made me a partner
their master who cares for them (referring to those who believed and did good deeds)
have not...? (expecting 'yes, it has' as answer)
her Lord (referring to the good word/tree mentioned before)
the worldly life (as opposed to the afterlife)
is it not true that... (expecting 'yes, it is true')
so that they cause (others) to stray
water (as the object being sent down from the sky)
a provision (that Allah brought forth from fruits as sustenance)
and he made (something) serve a purpose
so that it (the ship) may sail
by His command (referring to Allah's command making the ships sail)
and he forced to serve (the ships for you)
two (that are) constantly moving (describing the sun and moon)
and He controlled/subjected (the sun and moon)
you all asked for it (referring to everything Allah gave you)
indeed/truly/verily (introducing the statement: mankind is ungrateful)
the human (being acted upon in this ayah)
Ibrahim (as the subject who is speaking)
secure/safe (as a quality being given to the city)
and my sons (part of Ibrahim's prayer)
O Lord! (calling out to the Lord)
indeed they (feminine plural, referring to idols)
they (feminine) made (many people) lose the way
so indeed he (referring back to whoever follows)
One who is very forgiving (describing Allah)
Most Merciful (as an attribute of Allah)
in a valley (location where Abraham settled his family)
and provide them! (as a command to Allah)
they are thankful (referring to people in general who might be grateful for Allah's blessings)
and not (connecting to previous statement about what Allah knows)
of the sky, of the heaven
the one who (gave me Ismail and Isaac)
despite (my old age in this ayah)
Ismail (the son of Prophet Ibrahim mentioned in this ayah)
of the (specific) act of calling out/praying
make me! (command to Allah in prayer)
my offspring/descendants (speaking to Allah in prayer)
and for my parents (both of them)
she stares with eyes wide open
ones rushing forward with fixed gaze (describing multiple people)
their heads (referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier in the ayah)
an emptiness/void (referring to their hearts being completely empty of understanding)
we will be responded to (to your call)
those who (describing the people who wronged themselves)
and it became very clear (by itself)
we did (referring to what was done to those who wronged themselves)
His promise (that Allah will not break)
on that day (when the criminals will be bound together)
those who are firmly bound together
and she/it covers/overwhelms (and the fire covers)
that which (emphatic, connects 'every soul' to what it 'earned')
and so that they may be warned
and so that they may know
and so that he remembers/is reminded intensively
of the minds/intellects (referring to people of understanding)
